articles.title: Gold Rises Above $4,500 as Iran Deal Hopes Ease Inflation Fears
articles.summary: Gold rose as reports that the US and Iran reached a preliminary deal to extend their ceasefire eased expectations of higher-for-longer interest rates.

articles.title: US Stocks Extend Highs as Traders Await Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: US stocks modestly extended all-time highs as traders waited to see whether America and Iran could finally get a peace deal across the line and restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Aluminum Squeeze Deepens as Spreads Tighten and Inventories Drop
articles.summary: The aluminum market is facing a deepening shortage as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz throttles supply, with spot prices for the metal spiking while exchange inventories slump.

articles.title: Zurich Bank Looks to Brazil, Florida as It Expands in Americas
articles.summary: EFG International AG, a private-banking boutique based in Zurich, is expanding its 300-person team serving clients from the Americas with an emphasis on Brazil and South Florida.

articles.title: IndiGo Posts Unexpected Loss as Mideast Turmoil Lifts Costs
articles.summary: IndiGo, Asia’s largest low-cost carrier, reported an unexpected quarterly loss, amid cost pressures, operational disruptions and softer demand linked to the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Indonesia and India Intervene to Prop Up Weakening Currencies
articles.summary: Indonesia and India intervened on Friday to prop up their currencies, which are being pressured by an energy price spike.

articles.title: Japan Used Record $73.6 Billion to Support Yen in Past Month

articles.title: Dollar’s Monthly Rise Leaves Strategists Wary of More Gains
articles.summary: This month’s rally in the dollar, as traders priced in the prospect of higher US interest rates, is leaving Wall Street strategists wary of further gains.
